ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-13-2016 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-16-2016 Continued To Apr-13-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Phyllis Berenson for (executor) Geraldine Turner.

The Court stated that we are here to approve a petition for first and final report on waiver of account and for approval of a trust, that there are a few issues and asked if there was consideration to accomplish the controlled gift through a blocked account. Ms. Berenson stated that they anticipated management of the trust and distribution for the daughter's support and that it would be costly and time consuming to keep coming back to the court. The Court stated that an ex parte would be less onerous that the accountings required for the trust and that the question is whether it has been considered and if it could accomplish the same end with less administrative cost. Ms. Berenson stated that it was considered, that it is fine and that the executor is not bondable. The Court stated that if the money is in a blocked account bond would not be an issue. Ms. Berenson stated that it is unfortunate that the decedent did not get legal advice, that he used an online service for the will which was written in 2008, that the beneficiary had various issues, that she needs to and wants to be independent, that the funds need to be used to pay rent and expenses for her and that she receives benefits which would not be affected by the trust. The Court stated that the obstacle to the blocked account is what is the control that she remains clean for 3 years. Ms. Berenson stated that it is provided for in the trust. The Court asked if both structures were considered is it the preference to go with the trust. Ms. Berenson stated that the issue is the drug issue and the request of the father. The Court addressed the gifts to minors, the issues would be avoided if the gifts were made from bequests and that if they are made out of the estate 3401 has procedural hurdles which have not been met. Ms. Berenson stated that they are already adults and with the trust issue they thought it should be done this way. The Court stated that it is their choice, that it is a bit strange and that if it is to be done through distributions there needs to be an oath from the parents. Ms. Berenson stated that there is a problem with the 17 year old, that her mother cannot be trusted and cannot sign the oath. The Court stated that there needs to be a continuance for an amendment to at least deal with that, that they can choose to make other adjustments and that it will need to be re-noticed. The accounting petition was continued to April 13, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to consider the choice between the trust and a blocked account.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)
